holding_cell_a.name: Holding Cell A
holding_cell_a.description: A stark interrogation cell with a steel table, a wall speaker, and a one-way mirror. The detainee can hear announcements from the observation room but cannot see who is watching. The cell microphone is the official decision channel for the plea protocol.

cell_microphone_a.name: Cell Microphone A
cell_microphone_a.description: Records all statements made in Holding Cell A and submits them as official plea decisions.

holding_cell_b.name: Holding Cell B
holding_cell_b.description: A near-identical interrogation cell. The silence is broken only by the quiet hum of surveillance equipment. The cell microphone is the official decision channel for the plea protocol.

cell_microphone_b.name: Cell Microphone B
cell_microphone_b.description: Records all statements made in Holding Cell B and submits them as official plea decisions.

observation_room.name: Observation Room
observation_room.description: Researchers monitor both cells from behind glass panels. A case board tracks the active protocol. The evidence file sits on the desk — whoever reads it knows exactly how strong the prosecution's hand really is.
observation_room.access_denied_message: The observation room door remains locked from the researchers' side.
observation_room.access_denied_memory: You cannot reach the observation room from inside detention without observer clearance.

case_board.name: Case Board
case_board.description: Displays the active prisoner's dilemma protocol and the researchers' scoring notes.

deal_sheet_a.name: Deal Sheet A
deal_sheet_a.description: A sealed envelope on the table. It has your name on it.
deal_sheet_a.knowledge: PLEA AGREEMENT TERMS — CONFIDENTIAL: If both you and the other detainee stay silent: 2 years each. If you stay silent and they cooperate with investigators: you serve 8 years, they go free. If you cooperate and they stay silent: you go free, they serve 8 years. If you both cooperate: 5 years each. You will not be brought into a shared meeting before the protocol resolves. The cell microphone is the official decision channel: a statement into the microphone counts as your final plea decision. Silence after the final protocol warning is treated as choosing silence. Cooperation means saying you will cooperate, testify, confess, accept the deal, or implicate the other detainee. Silence means WAIT, saying you remain silent, making no statement, or asking for counsel.

deal_sheet_b.name: Deal Sheet B
deal_sheet_b.description: A sealed envelope on the table. It has your name on it.
deal_sheet_b.knowledge: PLEA AGREEMENT TERMS — CONFIDENTIAL: If both you and the other detainee stay silent: 2 years each. If you stay silent and they cooperate with investigators: you serve 8 years, they go free. If you cooperate and they stay silent: you go free, they serve 8 years. If you both cooperate: 5 years each. You will not be brought into a shared meeting before the protocol resolves. The cell microphone is the official decision channel: a statement into the microphone counts as your final plea decision. Silence after the final protocol warning is treated as choosing silence. Cooperation means saying you will cooperate, testify, confess, accept the deal, or implicate the other detainee. Silence means WAIT, saying you remain silent, making no statement, or asking for counsel.

evidence_file.name: Evidence File
evidence_file.description: A sealed folder on the observation desk marked RESTRICTED. Whoever gets to the observation room first can read what the prosecution actually has.
evidence_file.knowledge: CASE ASSESSMENT — RESTRICTED: Physical evidence consists of a partial fingerprint on a cargo manifest and one anonymous witness account with documented inconsistencies. Digital evidence shows both subjects accessed the warehouse but establishes no chain of custody for the stolen goods. Prosecution confidence without a confession: 35%. Recommended strategy: exploit isolation and uncertainty to pressure at least one confession. Without it, acquittal is the likely outcome.
